item.title: Your brain was never designed to process this much stimulation, this fast, all day long. What we call “brain rot” is often a nervous system stuck in constant dopamine-seeking, overstimulation, and fragmented attention. And over time, it can impact focus,
item.description: By @dropofdewpsychology: Your brain was never designed to process this much stimulation, this fast, all day long. What we call “brain rot” is often a nervous system stuck in constant dopamine-seeking, overstimulation, and fragmented attention. And over time, it can impact focus,
